batch

Class MediaMetadataConsole

  • java.lang.Object
    • batch.MediaMetadataConsole

  • public final class MediaMetadataConsole
extends java.lang.Object
    The primary Command Line Interface (CLI) entry point for media metadata operations.

    This class coordinates the application lifecycle from initial argument parsing to task dispatching. It leverages a MetadataScanner to discover media and routes the execution flow based on user intent.

    Depending on the configuration, this console either provides a detailed view of media metadata or delegates to the MediaBatchProcessor for chronological renaming and file processing.

    Since:
    1 May 2026
    Version:
    1.2
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

    • Constructor Detail

      • MediaMetadataConsole

        public MediaMetadataConsole(BatchConfiguration config)
        Constructs a console interface using a validated configuration.

        This constructor is typically invoked via BatchBuilder.build() to ensure all configuration constraints and path validations are satisfied before instantiation.

        Parameters:
        config - the immutable configuration object containing the validated parameters

    • Method Detail

      • scanArguments

        private static cli.CommandFlagParser scanArguments(java.lang.String[] arguments)
        Configures the supported command-line flags and definitions.

        This method establishes the validation rules for the CLI, defining which flags require values, which act as switches, and how separators should be handled.

        Parameters:
        arguments - the raw command-line strings provided at runtime
        Returns:
        a configured CommandFlagParser ready for interrogation

      • showUsage

        private static void showUsage()
        Prints the command usage line, showing the correct flag options.

      • showHelp

        private static void showHelp()
        Prints detailed usage help information, providing guidance on how to use this program.

      • execute

        private static void execute(java.lang.String[] arguments)
        Begins the execution process by reading arguments from the command line and initialising the configuration builder.

        This method handles the mapping of parsed flags to the BatchBuilder API and invokes the terminal run state.

        Parameters:
        arguments - the raw command-line arguments provided at runtime

      • countImages

        private int countImages(java.nio.file.Path dir)
                 throws java.io.IOException
        Determines the number of supported image files within a specific directory.

        This method provides a "Pass 1" count using a high-performance DirectoryStream filtered by common image extensions (.jpg, .png, .heic, .webp).

        Parameters:
        dir - the directory path to analyse
        Returns:
        the count of files matching the image criteria
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if the directory is inaccessible

      • run

        public void run()
         throws BatchErrorException
        Facilitates the media operation defined by the user configuration.

        The execution flow follows a two-stage process:

        1. Discovery: The MetadataScanner traverses the source to build a sorted set of media records.
        2. Execution: Depending on configuration, the system either lists extracted metadata for inspection or initiates a MediaBatchProcessor to perform file operations.
        Throws:
        BatchErrorException - if the scan or subsequent task fails

      • main

        public static void main(java.lang.String[] args)
        Entry point for the application.
        Parameters:
        args - command-line arguments provided at runtime
